name: Lumbini
description: Lumbini, located in present-day Nepal, is the birthplace of Siddhartha Gautama, the historical Buddha. It is one of the four holiest places in Buddhism, the others being Bodh Gaya, Sarnath, and Kushinagar. Lumbini holds immense significance for Buddhists worldwide, representing the starting point of the Buddha's life and his eventual enlightenment. The site is believed to be where Queen Maya Devi, while on her way to her maternal home, gave birth to Siddhartha under a Sal tree in the Lumbini Grove. The event is said to have been accompanied by miraculous occurrences, signifying the auspicious nature of the birth. The Ashoka Pillar, erected by Emperor Ashoka in 249 BCE, marks the exact spot of the Buddha's birth and provides crucial historical evidence. The Maya Devi Temple, dedicated to the Buddha's mother, stands as a focal point for pilgrims and contains ancient relics and sculptures. The surrounding gardens and monasteries contribute to the serene and spiritual atmosphere of the site. Lumbini is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, recognized for its outstanding universal value and its pivotal role in the history of Buddhism. It serves as a place of pilgrimage, meditation, and reflection for Buddhists and visitors from all over the world, fostering peace and understanding. The preservation and conservation of Lumbini are essential for future generations to connect with the origins of Buddhist teachings and philosophy. Lumbini is more than just a historical site; it is a living testament to the Buddha's legacy and his message of compassion and enlightenment.
subtitle: Birthplace of the Buddha.

keyMyths.title: Birth of Siddhartha
keyMyths.description: Queen Maya Devi gave birth to Siddhartha Gautama under a Sal tree in Lumbini Grove. It was a miraculous birth with auspicious signs.

keyMyths.title: Ashoka's Visit
keyMyths.description: Emperor Ashoka visited Lumbini and erected a pillar to mark the Buddha's birthplace. The pillar serves as historical evidence of the event.

sources.author: UNESCO
sources.description: Information on Lumbini's status as a World Heritage Site.
